name: Telecommunications
description: Telco AI regulation for network optimization, customer profiling, content moderation, and automated service management.

highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used as safety components in the management and operation of critical digital infrastructure, including telecommunications networks classified as essential services.
Transparency obligations for AI systems that interact with natural persons (chatbots), generate synthetic content, or perform emotion recognition — applicable to telco customer service AI.

sectorRequirements.requirement: AI systems managing critical telecommunications infrastructure must be classified and assessed as high-risk where they serve as safety components of essential network services.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 6, Annex III para 2; NIS2 Directive; MCMC Industry Code; NIST AI RMF

sectorRequirements.requirement: Implement transparency obligations for AI-powered customer service chatbots, virtual assistants, and automated call routing systems, clearly informing users of AI interaction.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 50; Malaysia NGAIGE Transparency Principle; Singapore MAIGF Stakeholder Interaction; China GenAI Art. 5

sectorRequirements.requirement: Ensure AI-driven customer profiling and targeted marketing comply with data protection requirements, with opt-out mechanisms and restrictions on automated decision-making affecting service terms.
sectorRequirements.source: GDPR Art. 22; EU AI Act Art. 86; Malaysia PDPA 2010; MCMC Consumer Protection Code

sectorRequirements.requirement: Maintain resilience and fallback procedures for AI-driven network management systems, ensuring service continuity when AI optimization systems experience failures or adversarial interference.
sectorRequirements.source: NIS2 Directive; NIST AI RMF MANAGE function; MCMC Quality of Service Standards; EU AI Act Art. 15

sectorRequirements.requirement: Conduct fairness assessments of AI systems used for service eligibility, credit checks, pricing personalization, and churn prediction to prevent discriminatory treatment of customer segments.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 10; Malaysia NGAIGE Fairness Principle; OECD AI Principles; UK AI Framework Fairness

sectorRequirements.requirement: Implement content moderation AI governance covering accuracy monitoring, appeal mechanisms, and regular audits of automated content filtering and blocking systems.
sectorRequirements.source: EU Digital Services Act; China GenAI Art. 4, Art. 9; MCMC Content Code; UK Online Safety Act

sectorRequirements.requirement: Assess and document the societal impact of AI-driven network optimization decisions that may affect digital access equity, including coverage prioritization algorithms and service quality differentiation.
sectorRequirements.source: OECD AI Principles Inclusive Growth; UNESCO AI Ethics; Malaysia NGAIGE Inclusiveness; EU AI Act Recital 27

keyRisks.risk: Network optimization single point of failure
keyRisks.description: AI-driven network management may create single points of failure where model errors or adversarial attacks cause widespread service outages.
AI-based customer segmentation and pricing models may inadvertently discriminate against customers based on location, demographics, or usage patterns correlated with protected characteristics.
Automated content filtering may over-block legitimate communications or under-block harmful content, with errors amplified by the volume of telco traffic.
AI-optimized network investment decisions may deprioritize underserved areas, widening the digital divide between urban and rural or affluent and disadvantaged communities.
AI analytics on telecommunications metadata may enable mass surveillance capabilities that threaten privacy and civil liberties.
